label.purpose: Uppercase eyebrow labels with wide tracking.

numeral.purpose: Card values and revealed estimates; always tabular-nums.

shadows.name: card-hover-lift
shadows.purpose: Blue-tinted cast shadow as a vote card rises 8px on hover.

shadows.name: card-selected-glow
shadows.purpose: Two-layer glow + cast shadow on the player's selected card (lifted 12px). Strongest elevation in the system.

motion.name: card-transition
motion.value: transform 0.2s ease, border-color 0.2s ease, color 0.2s ease, box-shadow 0.2s ease

motion.name: dist-bar-grow
motion.purpose: Distribution bar width animation on reveal.

extensions.reducedMotion: Global @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) collapses all animation/transition durations to 0.01ms so the reveal resolves instantly without motion.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single gradient-filled call to action (Create Room, reveal actions).
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { width: 100%; padding: 12px; border: none; border-radius: 10px; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; color: #fff; background: linear-gradient(135deg, #3b82f6, #8b5cf6); cursor: pointer; transition: opacity 0.15s, transform 0.15s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { opacity: 0.9; transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #3b82f6; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.4; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: The quiet flat sibling to the primary action (Join Room).
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { width: 100%; padding: 12px; border: none; border-radius: 10px; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; color: #e5e5e5; background: #262626; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.15s, transform 0.15s; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #333333; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #3b82f6; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-secondary:disabled { opacity: 0.4; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Void-filled field on a Surface panel; doubled focus treatment for dark legibility.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; padding: 12px 16px; background: #0a0a0a; border: 1px solid #333333; border-radius: 10px; color: #e5e5e5; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 15px; transition: border-color 0.2s; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #525252; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #3b82f6; outline: 2px solid #3b82f6; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Vote Card
components.description: Signature component: a tall playing card cut from the navy deck. Lifts 8px on hover.
components.css: .ds-vote-card { width: 68px; height: 96px; border: 2px solid #2a2a4a; border-radius: 12px; background: linear-gradient(145deg, #1a1a2e, #16213e); color: #a3a3a3; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 26px; font-weight: 800; cursor: pointer; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; transition: transform 0.2s ease, border-color 0.2s ease, color 0.2s ease, box-shadow 0.2s ease; } .ds-vote-card:hover:not(:disabled) { transform: translateY(-8px); border-color: #3b82f6; color: #60a5fa; box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(59, 130, 246, 0.15); } .ds-vote-card:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #3b82f6; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-vote-card:disabled { opacity: 0.4; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Vote Card (Selected)
components.description: The card the player has committed: deeper navy, lifted 12px, glowing. The card visibly leaves the deck.
components.css: .ds-vote-card-selected { width: 68px; height: 96px; border: 2px solid #3b82f6; border-radius: 12px; background: linear-gradient(145deg, #0f2440, #1e3a5f); color: #60a5fa; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 26px; font-weight: 800; cursor: pointer; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; transform: translateY(-12px); box-shadow: 0 0 20px rgba(96, 165, 250, 0.2), 0 12px 32px rgba(59, 130, 246, 0.2); }

components.name: Estimate Card
components.description: A revealed estimate: flatter and quieter than a vote card, because the choosing is over and now it is data.
components.css: .ds-estimate-card { width: 64px; padding: 16px 0; border: 2px solid #333333; border-radius: 10px; background: #171717; color: #a3a3a3; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 26px; font-weight: 800; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; }

components.name: Distribution Bar
components.description: Reveal-phase histogram row: value, gradient-filled track, count. Tabular numerals.
components.html:
5
3
components.css: .ds-dist-row { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-dist-value { width: 24px; text-align: right; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 700; color: #e5e5e5; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-dist-track { flex: 1; height: 14px; background: #0a0a0a; border-radius: 7px; overflow: hidden; } .ds-dist-bar { height: 100%; background: linear-gradient(90deg, #3b82f6, #8b5cf6); border-radius: 5px; transition: width 0.4s ease; } .ds-dist-count { width: 20px; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; color: #a3a3a3; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; }

narrative.overview: estimate-it has two emotional beats, and the design serves both. During the vote it is a quiet room: a near-black surface, muted neutrals, no movement, nothing that could leak or nudge another person's number. Then everyone reveals at once, and the surface comes alive: cards lift, the blue->violet signature gradient blooms across the stats, the distribution bars animate in, success-green confirms consensus. Focus during concentration, play once the decision is made. The metaphor is a real planning-poker table: voting cards are tall, physical playing cards cut from a dark navy deck that lift on hover and glow when chosen.
keyCharacteristics: Dark-only, near-black canvas (#0a0a0a) built for screen-shared remote sessions
Two-phase rhythm: still and muted during the vote, animated and bright at reveal
A physical card deck with its own navy palette as the signature object
A blue->violet gradient as the recurring brand signal
Space Grotesk throughout, geometric and a touch playful
Tabular numerals everywhere a value is compared

rules.name: The Deck-Is-Separate Rule
rules.body: The navy deck palette (#1a1a2e family) is reserved exclusively for voting and estimate cards. Never paint chrome, panels, or buttons in deck navy, and never paint a card in neutral gray.

rules.name: The Two-Beat Color Rule
rules.body: During the vote, the surface stays neutral and muted; saturated color is held back. The signature gradient and Consensus Green arrive at reveal. Color is a reward for finishing the vote, not ambient decoration.

rules.name: The Tabular Rule
rules.body: Every number a human compares against another number uses tabular-nums. Estimates must align in a column at a glance.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: Space Grotesk does all the work. Do not introduce a second display or mono face; reach for weight and the uppercase label treatment instead.

rules.name: The Earned-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Shadows are forbidden at rest. They exist only as a response to hover and selection on cards.

dos: Do keep the vote phase quiet: neutral surfaces, no saturated color, no motion. Save the signature gradient and Consensus Green for the reveal.
Do reserve the navy deck palette (#1a1a2e family) for voting and estimate cards only.
Do use tabular-nums on every compared value.
Do keep elevation off resting surfaces; let card hover (-8px) and selection (-12px) be the only shadows.
Do tint neutrals cool toward the deck; never use pure #000 or #fff.
Do give every interactive element the 2px Signal-Blue focus outline, and honor prefers-reduced-motion.
donts: Don't build dense, Jira-style boards or busy multi-gradient dashboards.
Don't ship identical icon-heading-text card grids or hero-metric templates.
Don't use the blue->violet gradient as text via background-clip: text, except the single existing wordmark dot.
Don't add border-left/border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe.
Don't paint chrome or buttons in deck navy, or paint a vote card in neutral gray.
Don't introduce a second font family.
Don't animate during the vote or leak vote state through any pre-reveal visual cue.
